jokes.text: Tony's panicked reaction to jury duty - 'Oh, no! No! Ah, why me?'
jokes.setup: Tony gets called for jury duty, which most would see as civic duty
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top horror at the prospect
jokes.explanation: Standard character-based comedy - Tony's simple-minded fear of responsibility comes through clearly

jokes.text: Alex's immediate refusal to talk to his father without explanation
jokes.setup: Someone's father is calling - normally you'd take the call
jokes.punchline: Alex's flat 'I don't want to talk to him' with no context
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that immediately establishes tension and gets a laugh from the unexpectedness

jokes.text: Alex offering to trade talking to his father for Tony's jury duty
jokes.setup: Tony dreads jury duty, Alex dreads talking to his father
jokes.punchline: Alex would rather face the justice system than his dad
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel that reveals character depth while getting a solid laugh - well-constructed

jokes.text: Jim's dismissive 'Well, you know, some people yakkety-yakkety-yakkety-yak'
jokes.setup: Alex explains he and his father have only talked once in 20 years
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual dismissal of communication as pointless chatter
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Jim's dismissive attitude with good verbal rhythm

jokes.text: Louie's jury peer joke: 'Who are they tryin', a pinhead?'
jokes.setup: Legal principle about jury of peers
jokes.punchline: Louie suggesting Tony's peers would be pinheads
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie insult that's mean but funny, well-timed after Tony's jury duty panic

jokes.text: Louie's elaborate jury scenario ending with 'there's Banta playin' with his lips'
jokes.setup: Building tension about a serious legal case with high stakes
jokes.punchline: The devastating image of Tony mindlessly playing with his lips as juror
jokes.explanation: Excellent build-up to a perfectly specific visual punchline that captures Tony's vacant nature

jokes.text: 'Since when?' '1974.' Alex hasn't seen his father who's lived nearby for years
jokes.setup: Father wants to reconnect, lives in town now
jokes.punchline: He's been there for years and they've never met
jokes.explanation: Sharp reveal that makes the estrangement even more absurd - they're geographically close but emotionally distant

jokes.text: 'My God, who is this man?' Elaine's shock at Joe's behavior
jokes.setup: Learning about Joe's womanizing past
jokes.punchline: Elaine's dramatic moral outrage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Elaine's tendency to dramatize situations

jokes.text: 'What am I gonna do with a sweet, little old man?' Alex dreading reconciliation even if Joe has changed
jokes.setup: Suggestion that his father might have mellowed with age
jokes.punchline: Alex finding even a reformed father problematic
jokes.explanation: Good character insight showing Alex's deep ambivalence about any relationship with his father

jokes.text: Alex's description of their awkward greeting ritual - not knowing whether to shake hands or hug
jokes.setup: Describing their reunion meetings
jokes.punchline: The specific awkwardness of not knowing the proper greeting
jokes.explanation: Relatable, specific observation about family awkwardness that many can identify with

jokes.text: 'You push a girl like that away?' Joe's immediate interest in Elaine
jokes.setup: Tense father-son reunion moment
jokes.punchline: Joe immediately distracted by attractive woman
jokes.explanation: Establishes Joe's character perfectly - even in emotional moments, he's distracted by women

jokes.text: Jim's overly emotional hug declaration: 'That's for all the sons in the world who never hugged their fathers'
jokes.setup: Awkward father-son reunion
jokes.punchline: Jim making it about all fathers and sons everywhere
jokes.explanation: Jim's well-meaning but overly dramatic gesture makes an awkward situation more awkward

jokes.text: 'I didn't say "perfect," I said "good."' Joe's response to Alex's criticism
jokes.setup: Alex points out Joe never sent money
jokes.punchline: Joe's semantic defense of his character
jokes.explanation: Sharp line showing Joe's ability to deflect criticism with wordplay

jokes.text: Joe's description of his ideal woman perfectly describing Karen who's sitting right there
jokes.setup: Joe describing his ideal woman in abstract terms
jokes.punchline: Camera reveals he's been watching Karen who fits the description exactly
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed visual comedy that shows Joe's predatory nature while setting up the plot

jokes.text: 'I memorized her license plate' - Joe's preparation for approaching Karen
jokes.setup: Joe spotted Karen before Alex did
jokes.punchline: The creepy level of preparation he put into approaching her
jokes.explanation: Funny and slightly disturbing detail that perfectly captures Joe's approach to women

jokes.text: Alex's honest admission: 'I hate jazz anyway. I was just foolishly trying to show you that I have a mind of my own'
jokes.setup: Alex went along with Joe's jazz suggestion
jokes.punchline: His brutally honest admission about trying to assert independence
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Alex's self-awareness about his father's influence on him

jokes.text: 'Smooth, Reiger, smooth' - Louie's sarcastic commentary
jokes.setup: Alex successfully gets a date with an attractive woman
jokes.punchline: Louie's begrudging, sarcastic acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie - can't give a compliment without sarcasm, but perfect character consistency

jokes.text: Joe's pickup line to Karen: 'I find it hard to resist being cute. I even went to Cutie-Pies Anonymous'
jokes.setup: Joe approaching Karen with an apology for being forward
jokes.punchline: The absurd support group for people who are too cute
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both self-deprecating and charming - shows Joe's skill with women

jokes.text: Karen's response: 'I must say, I do feel like dunking you in a cup of coffee'
jokes.setup: Joe's cutie-pie line
jokes.punchline: Karen's clever comeback about dunking cute things in coffee
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback that shows Karen can match Joe's wit while playing on the cute/cookie theme

jokes.text: Joe's logic about dating both father and son: the father-son relationship caveat
jokes.setup: Karen expressing concern about dating both men
jokes.punchline: Joe minimizing the family connection to justify it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joe's ability to rationalize questionable behavior

jokes.text: 'Which one of you is that?' when Joe mentions the 'harmless old man'
jokes.setup: Joe describing himself as a harmless old man
jokes.punchline: Karen's innocent question that reveals she doesn't see him as old/harmless
jokes.explanation: Nice reversal that shows Karen sees Joe as anything but harmless, feeding his ego

jokes.text: 'Reiger's on thin ice, and I'm a blow torch' - Louie's metaphor
jokes.setup: Someone asks why Louie is amused
jokes.punchline: Louie's colorful metaphor for being about to expose Alex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie line - vivid, mean-spirited, and perfectly captures his anticipation of Alex's downfall

jokes.text: Tony's residence length answer: 'About 30 feet' instead of time duration
jokes.setup: Jury questionnaire asks length of residence
jokes.punchline: Tony interpreting 'length' as physical measurement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Tony's literal-minded nature - classic Taxi comedy

jokes.text: 'Uh, maybe you measured wrong' - response to Tony's confusion
jokes.setup: Tony's absurd misunderstanding of 'length of residence'
jokes.punchline: Alex's practical suggestion missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that compounds Tony's confusion with deadpan practicality

jokes.text: Louie's wine metaphor buildup: 'It's like a fine wine. First, we should uncork it...'
jokes.setup: Louie promising to reveal information about Alex
jokes.punchline: Elaborate wine tasting metaphor for gossip
jokes.explanation: Good character bit showing Louie's love of dragging out drama with pretentious metaphors

jokes.text: 'to share a woman... with his own father' - Louie's revelation
jokes.setup: Building up Alex's 'broad-mindedness'
jokes.punchline: The devastating family connection revelation
jokes.explanation: Excellent revelation that lands perfectly - the timing, setup, and payoff are all expertly crafted

jokes.text: 'Yeah, me and my mother' - Alex's bitter response about mistaking Joe for his father
jokes.setup: Karen suggests someone mistook Joe for Alex's father
jokes.punchline: Alex's dark humor about his mother making the same mistake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that reveals family history while getting a great laugh - excellent writing

jokes.text: 'That munchkin I was having lunch here with the other day is my father!'
jokes.setup: Karen's confusion about Joe's identity
jokes.punchline: Alex's exasperated revelation using 'munchkin' for his father
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Alex's frustrated use of 'munchkin' showing his view of his father

jokes.text: 'My mother thought I had asthma' - Alex explaining his breathless reaction to his father's beautiful women
jokes.setup: Alex describing being in awe of his father's women
jokes.punchline: His mother misdiagnosing his awestruck breathlessness as asthma
jokes.explanation: Excellent line that perfectly captures childhood confusion and family dysfunction with great specificity

jokes.text: 'He imitates soup' followed by 'Pea soup'
jokes.setup: Karen trying to explain what makes Joe funny
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous specificity of imitating pea soup
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail that shows Joe's weird charm while being genuinely funny

jokes.text: 'he loves women' vs 'But I love women' - 'No, no, no... I mean, he loves women'
jokes.setup: Alex claiming he loves women too
jokes.punchline: Karen's distinction between different types of 'loving women'
jokes.explanation: Excellent distinction that gets at the difference between Alex and Joe's relationship with women

jokes.text: 'makes me almost wish he wasn't slime. Now wait a minute, that's my father you're talking about. Please continue.'
jokes.setup: Karen calling Joe slime, Alex defending his father
jokes.punchline: Alex immediately wanting to hear more criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Alex's conflicted feelings - he defends Joe but wants to hear more dirt

jokes.text: 'No, I was thinking more of my face and his face' - Alex wanting to confront Joe directly
jokes.setup: Karen suggesting he call Joe to confront him
jokes.punchline: Alex preferring physical confrontation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex finally ready to stand up to his father

jokes.text: 'I always wanted a fish tank; they're so pretty. But the fish are too much bother'
jokes.setup: Joe mentioning his empty fish tank
jokes.punchline: His lazy reasoning for having a tank with no fish
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Joe's character - wants the aesthetic but not the responsibility

jokes.text: 'She always used a little too much salt' vs Alex's defense of the salt level
jokes.setup: Joe critiquing his ex-wife's cooking
jokes.punchline: Alex's passionate defense of his mother's salt usage
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that shows how deep the family wounds go - even salt levels become emotional

jokes.text: Joe's manipulation: 'You have to see me. You have to listen to me, 'cause I'm old. I'm going to die.'
jokes.setup: Alex threatening to cut off contact
jokes.punchline: Joe's shameless use of mortality as emotional blackmail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Joe's manipulative skills - dark but perfectly in character

jokes.text: Joe demonstrates the pea soup imitation: 'Thick pea soup coming to a furious boil'
jokes.setup: Earlier reference to Joe imitating pea soup
jokes.punchline: The actual performance of this absurd routine
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that delivers on the earlier absurd promise with physical comedy

jokes.text: 'You can't do that without getting a pinch on the cheek'
jokes.setup: Joe demonstrating his pea soup impression
jokes.punchline: His confidence that it inevitably leads to affection
jokes.explanation: Shows Joe's supreme confidence in his charm techniques - both funny and revealing

jokes.text: 'I'm not gonna kiss you on top of the head. Your loss.'
jokes.setup: Joe telling Alex to kiss his head
jokes.punchline: Joe's casual dismissal of Alex's refusal
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending note showing Joe's unshakeable confidence and Alex's continued resistance
